---
title: 'Benchmark: Projection Pursuit Regression (PPR)'
author: 'Kenichi Satoh'
date: "Last updated: `r format(Sys.time(), '%Y/%m/%d')`"
output:
  word_document:
    fig_width: 12
    fig_height: 12
    toc: no
---

This script reproduces the PPR benchmark results in Section 4.3
of "Wild Bootstrap Inference for Non-Negative Matrix Factorization
with Random Effects" (Satoh, 2026).

```{r, dpi=300, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=TRUE}

# Load OD flow data (pre-saved to avoid e-stat download issues)
load("data/od_data.RData")
cat("Loaded OD data: Y =", dim(Y_od), ", A =", dim(A_od), "\n")

Y <- Y_od; A <- A_od
hub.index <- c(13, 23, 27, 40)

## --- PPR (Projection Pursuit Regression) ---
## Observations = destinations (47), predictors = hubs (4), responses = non-hubs (43)

X <- t(A)  # 47 x 4
Yresp <- t(Y)  # 47 x 43

colnames(X) <- c("Tokyo","Aichi","Osaka","Fukuoka")

set.seed(1)
fit_ppr <- ppr(x = X, y = Yresp,
               nterms = 4,
               max.terms = 10,
               sm.method = "supsmu")

summary(fit_ppr)

## Predictions (in-sample)
Yhat <- predict(fit_ppr, newdata = X)  # 47 x 43

## Multivariate R-squared
rss <- sum((Yresp - Yhat)^2)
tss <- sum((Yresp - matrix(colMeans(Yresp),
                           nrow(Yresp), ncol(Yresp), byrow=TRUE))^2)
R2_all <- 1 - rss/tss
R2_all

## Ridge function plots
par(mfrow=c(2,2))
plot(fit_ppr)

```
